NKJV.1: A Contemplation of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the mercies of the LORD forever; With my mouth will I make known Your faithfulness to all generations.
NKJV.2: For I have said, “Mercy shall be built up forever;  Your faithfulness You shall establish in the very heavens.”
NKJV.3: “I  have made a covenant with My chosen, I have sworn to My servant David:
NKJV.4: ‘Your seed I will establish forever, And build up your throne to all generations.’ ” Selah
NKJV.5: And the heavens will praise Your wonders, O LORD; Your faithfulness also in the assembly of the saints.
NKJV.6: For who in the heavens can be compared to the LORD? Who among the sons of the mighty can be likened to the LORD?
NKJV.7: God is greatly to be feared in the assembly of the saints, And to be held in reverence by all those around Him.
NKJV.8: O LORD God of hosts, Who is mighty like You, O LORD? Your faithfulness also surrounds You.
NKJV.9: You rule the raging of the sea; When its waves rise, You still them.
NKJV.10: You have broken Rahab in pieces, as one who is slain; You have scattered Your enemies with Your mighty arm.
NKJV.11: The heavens are Yours, the earth also is Yours; The world and all its fullness, You have founded them.
NKJV.12: The north and the south, You have created them;  Tabor and Hermon rejoice in Your name.
NKJV.13: You have a mighty arm; Strong is Your hand, and high is Your right hand.
NKJV.14: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne; Mercy and truth go before Your face.
NKJV.15: Blessed are the people who know the joyful sound! They walk, O LORD, in the light of Your countenance.
NKJV.16: In Your name they rejoice all day long, And in Your righteousness they are exalted.
NKJV.17: For You are the glory of their strength, And in Your favor our horn is exalted.
NKJV.18: For our shield belongs to the LORD, And our king to the Holy One of Israel.
NKJV.19: Then You spoke in a vision to Your holy one, And said: “I have given help to one who is mighty; I have exalted one chosen from the people.
NKJV.20: I have found My servant David; With My holy oil I have anointed him,
NKJV.21: With whom My hand shall be established; Also My arm shall strengthen him.
NKJV.22: The enemy shall not outwit him, Nor the son of wickedness afflict him.
NKJV.23: I will beat down his foes before his face, And plague those who hate him.
NKJV.24: “But My faithfulness and My mercy shall be with him, And in My name his horn shall be exalted.
NKJV.25: Also I will set his hand over the sea, And his right hand over the rivers.
NKJV.26: He shall cry to Me, ‘You are my Father, My God, and the rock of my salvation.’
NKJV.27: Also I will make him My firstborn, The highest of the kings of the earth.
NKJV.28: My mercy I will keep for him forever, And My covenant shall stand firm with him.
NKJV.29: His seed also I will make to endure forever, And his throne as the days of heaven.
NKJV.30: “If  his sons forsake My law And do not walk in My judgments,
NKJV.31: If they break My statutes And do not keep My commandments,
NKJV.32: Then I will punish their transgression with the rod, And their iniquity with stripes.
NKJV.33: Nevertheless My lovingkindness I will not utterly take from him, Nor allow My faithfulness to fail.
NKJV.34: My covenant I will not break, Nor alter the word that has gone out of My lips.
NKJV.35: Once I have sworn by My holiness; I will not lie to David:
NKJV.36: His seed shall endure forever, And his throne as the sun before Me;
NKJV.37: It shall be established forever like the moon, Even like the faithful witness in the sky.” Selah
NKJV.38: But You have cast off and abhorred, You have been furious with Your anointed.
NKJV.39: You have renounced the covenant of Your servant; You have profaned his crown by casting it to the ground.
NKJV.40: You have broken down all his hedges; You have brought his strongholds to ruin.
NKJV.41: All who pass by the way plunder him; He is a reproach to his neighbors.
NKJV.42: You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries; You have made all his enemies rejoice.
NKJV.43: You have also turned back the edge of his sword, And have not sustained him in the battle.
NKJV.44: You have made his glory cease, And cast his throne down to the ground.
NKJV.45: The days of his youth You have shortened; You have covered him with shame. Selah
NKJV.46: How long, LORD? Will You hide Yourself forever? Will Your wrath burn like fire?
NKJV.47: Remember how short my time is; For what futility have You created all the children of men?
NKJV.48: What man can live and not see death? Can he deliver his life from the power of the grave? Selah
NKJV.49: Lord, where are Your former lovingkindnesses, Which You swore to David in Your truth?
NKJV.50: Remember, Lord, the reproach of Your servants— How I bear in my bosom the reproach of all the many peoples,
NKJV.51: With which Your enemies have reproached, O LORD, With which they have reproached the footsteps of Your anointed.
NKJV.52: Blessed be the LORD forevermore! Amen and Amen.

ESV.1: I will sing of the steadfast love of the LORD, forever; with my mouth I will make known your faithfulness to all generations.
ESV.2: For I said, “Steadfast love will be built up forever; in the heavens you will establish your faithfulness.”
ESV.3: You have said, “I have made a covenant with my chosen one; I have sworn to David my servant:
ESV.4: ‘I will establish your offspring forever, and build your throne for all generations.’” Selah
ESV.5: Let the heavens praise your wonders, O LORD, your faithfulness in the assembly of the holy ones!
ESV.6: For who in the skies can be compared to the LORD? Who among the heavenly beings  is like the LORD,
ESV.7: a God greatly to be feared in the council of the holy ones, and awesome above all who are around him?
ESV.8: O LORD God of hosts, who is mighty as you are, O LORD, with your faithfulness all around you?
ESV.9: You rule the raging of the sea; when its waves rise, you still them.
ESV.10: You crushed Rahab like a carcass; you scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.
ESV.11: The heavens are yours; the earth also is yours; the world and all that is in it, you have founded them.
ESV.12: The north and the south, you have created them; Tabor and Hermon joyously praise your name.
ESV.13: You have a mighty arm; strong is your hand, high your right hand.
ESV.14: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne; steadfast love and faithfulness go before you.
ESV.15: Blessed are the people who know the festal shout, who walk, O LORD, in the light of your face,
ESV.16: who exult in your name all the day and in your righteousness are exalted.
ESV.17: For you are the glory of their strength; by your favor our horn is exalted.
ESV.18: For our shield belongs to the LORD, our king to the Holy One of Israel.
ESV.19: Of old you spoke in a vision to your godly one,  and said: “I have granted help to one who is mighty; I have exalted one chosen from the people.
ESV.20: I have found David, my servant; with my holy oil I have anointed him,
ESV.21: so that my hand shall be established with him; my arm also shall strengthen him.
ESV.22: The enemy shall not outwit him; the wicked shall not humble him.
ESV.23: I will crush his foes before him and strike down those who hate him.
ESV.24: My faithfulness and my steadfast love shall be with him, and in my name shall his horn be exalted.
ESV.25: I will set his hand on the sea and his right hand on the rivers.
ESV.26: He shall cry to me, ‘You are my Father, my God, and the Rock of my salvation.’
ESV.27: And I will make him the firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.
ESV.28: My steadfast love I will keep for him forever, and my covenant will stand firm  for him.
ESV.29: I will establish his offspring forever and his throne as the days of the heavens.
ESV.30: If his children forsake my law and do not walk according to my rules,
ESV.31: if they violate my statutes and do not keep my commandments,
ESV.32: then I will punish their transgression with the rod and their iniquity with stripes,
ESV.33: but I will not remove from him my steadfast love or be false to my faithfulness.
ESV.34: I will not violate my covenant or alter the word that went forth from my lips.
ESV.35: Once for all I have sworn by my holiness; I will not lie to David.
ESV.36: His offspring shall endure forever, his throne as long as the sun before me.
ESV.37: Like the moon it shall be established forever, a faithful witness in the skies.” Selah
ESV.38: But now you have cast off and rejected; you are full of wrath against your anointed.
ESV.39: You have renounced the covenant with your servant; you have defiled his crown in the dust.
ESV.40: You have breached all his walls; you have laid his strongholds in ruins.
ESV.41: All who pass by plunder him; he has become the scorn of his neighbors.
ESV.42: You have exalted the right hand of his foes; you have made all his enemies rejoice.
ESV.43: You have also turned back the edge of his sword, and you have not made him stand in battle.
ESV.44: You have made his splendor to cease and cast his throne to the ground.
ESV.45: You have cut short the days of his youth; you have covered him with shame. Selah
ESV.46: How long, O LORD? Will you hide yourself forever? How long will your wrath burn like fire?
ESV.47: Remember how short my time is! For what vanity you have created all the children of man!
ESV.48: What man can live and never see death? Who can deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah
ESV.49: Lord, where is your steadfast love of old, which by your faithfulness you swore to David?
ESV.50: Remember, O Lord, how your servants are mocked, and how I bear in my heart the insults  of all the many nations,
ESV.51: with which your enemies mock, O LORD, with which they mock the footsteps of your anointed.
ESV.52: Blessed be the LORD forever! Amen and Amen.

NASB.1: A Maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the lovingkindness of the LORD forever; To all generations I will make known Your faithfulness with my mouth.
NASB.2: For I have said, 'Lovingkindness will be built up forever; In the heavens You will establish Your faithfulness.'
NASB.3: 'I have made a covenant with My chosen; I have sworn to David My servant,
NASB.4: I will establish your seed forever And build up your throne to all generations.' Selah.
NASB.5: The heavens will praise Your wonders, O LORD; Your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.
NASB.6: For who in the skies is comparable to the LORD? Who among the sons of the mighty is like the LORD,
NASB.7: A God greatly feared in the council of the holy ones, And awesome above all those who are around Him?
NASB.8: O LORD God of hosts, who is like You, O mighty LORD? Your faithfulness also surrounds You.
NASB.9: You rule the swelling of the sea; When its waves rise, You still them.
NASB.10: You Yourself crushed Rahab like one who is slain; You scattered Your enemies with Your mighty arm.
NASB.11: The heavens are Yours, the earth also is Yours; The world and all it contains, You have founded them.
NASB.12: The north and the south, You have created them; Tabor and Hermon shout for joy at Your name.
NASB.13: You have a strong arm; Your hand is mighty, Your right hand is exalted.
NASB.14: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne; Lovingkindness and truth go before You.
NASB.15: How blessed are the people who know the joyful sound! O LORD, they walk in the light of Your countenance.
NASB.16: In Your name they rejoice all the day, And by Your righteousness they are exalted.
NASB.17: For You are the glory of their strength, And by Your favor our horn is exalted.
NASB.18: For our shield belongs to the LORD, And our king to the Holy One of Israel.
NASB.19: Once You spoke in vision to Your godly ones, And said, 'I have given help to one who is mighty; I have exalted one chosen from the people.
NASB.20: 'I have found David My servant; With My holy oil I have anointed him,
NASB.21: With whom My hand will be established; My arm also will strengthen him.
NASB.22: 'The enemy will not deceive him, Nor the son of wickedness afflict him.
NASB.23: 'But I shall crush his adversaries before him, And strike those who hate him.
NASB.24: 'My faithfulness and My lovingkindness will be with him, And in My name his horn will be exalted.
NASB.25: 'I shall also set his hand on the sea And his right hand on the rivers.
NASB.26: 'He will cry to Me, 'You are my Father, My God, and the rock of my salvation.'
NASB.27: 'I also shall make him [My] firstborn, The highest of the kings of the earth.
NASB.28: 'My lovingkindness I will keep for him forever, And My covenant shall be confirmed to him.
NASB.29: 'So I will establish his descendants forever And his throne as the days of heaven.
NASB.30: 'If his sons forsake My law And do not walk in My judgments,
NASB.31: If they violate My statutes And do not keep My commandments,
NASB.32: Then I will punish their transgression with the rod And their iniquity with stripes.
NASB.33: 'But I will not break off My lovingkindness from him, Nor deal falsely in My faithfulness.
NASB.34: 'My covenant I will not violate, Nor will I alter the utterance of My lips.
NASB.35: 'Once I have sworn by My holiness; I will not lie to David.
NASB.36: 'His descendants shall endure forever And his throne as the sun before Me.
NASB.37: 'It shall be established forever like the moon, And the witness in the sky is faithful.' Selah.
NASB.38: But You have cast off and rejected, You have been full of wrath against Your anointed.
NASB.39: You have spurned the covenant of Your servant; You have profaned his crown in the dust.
NASB.40: You have broken down all his walls; You have brought his strongholds to ruin.
NASB.41: All who pass along the way plunder him; He has become a reproach to his neighbors.
NASB.42: You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries; You have made all his enemies rejoice.
NASB.43: You also turn back the edge of his sword And have not made him stand in battle.
NASB.44: You have made his splendor to cease And cast his throne to the ground.
NASB.45: You have shortened the days of his youth; You have covered him with shame. Selah.
NASB.46: How long, O LORD? Will You hide Yourself forever? Will Your wrath burn like fire?
NASB.47: Remember what my span of life is; For what vanity You have created all the sons of men!
NASB.48: What man can live and not see death? Can he deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah.
NASB.49: Where are Your former lovingkindnesses, O Lord, Which You swore to David in Your faithfulness?
NASB.50: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of Your servants; How I bear in my bosom [the reproach of] all the many peoples,
NASB.51: With which Your enemies have reproached, O LORD, With which they have reproached the footsteps of Your anointed.
NASB.52: Blessed be the LORD forever! Amen and Amen.

NLT.1:
I will sing of the LORD’s unfailing love forever!
Young and old will hear of your faithfulness.
NLT.2:
Your unfailing love will last forever.
Your faithfulness is as enduring as the heavens.
NLT.3:
The LORD said, “I have made a covenant with David, my chosen servant.
I have sworn this oath to him:
NLT.4:
‘I will establish your descendants as kings forever;
they will sit on your throne from now until eternity.’” Interlude
NLT.5:
All heaven will praise your great wonders, LORD;
myriads of angels will praise you for your faithfulness.
NLT.6:
For who in all of heaven can compare with the LORD?
What mightiest angel is anything like the LORD?
NLT.7:
The highest angelic powers stand in awe of God.
He is far more awesome than all who surround his throne.
NLT.8:
O LORD God of Heaven’s Armies!
Where is there anyone as mighty as you, O LORD?
You are entirely faithful.
NLT.9:
You rule the oceans.
You subdue their storm-tossed waves.
NLT.10:
You crushed the great sea monster.
You scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.
NLT.11:
The heavens are yours, and the earth is yours;
everything in the world is yours — you created it all.
NLT.12:
You created north and south.
Mount Tabor and Mount Hermon praise your name.
NLT.13:
Powerful is your arm!
Strong is your hand!
Your right hand is lifted high in glorious strength.
NLT.14:
Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne.
Unfailing love and truth walk before you as attendants.
NLT.15:
Happy are those who hear the joyful call to worship,
for they will walk in the light of your presence, LORD.
NLT.16:
They rejoice all day long in your wonderful reputation.
They exult in your righteousness.
NLT.17:
You are their glorious strength.
It pleases you to make us strong.
NLT.18:
Yes, our protection comes from the LORD,
and he, the Holy One of Israel, has given us our king.
NLT.19:
Long ago you spoke in a vision to your faithful people.
You said, “I have raised up a warrior.
I have selected him from the common people to be king.
NLT.20:
I have found my servant David.
I have anointed him with my holy oil.
NLT.21:
I will steady him with my hand;
with my powerful arm I will make him strong.
NLT.22:
His enemies will not defeat him,
nor will the wicked overpower him.
NLT.23:
I will beat down his adversaries before him
and destroy those who hate him.
NLT.24:
My faithfulness and unfailing love will be with him,
and by my authority he will grow in power.
NLT.25:
I will extend his rule over the sea,
his dominion over the rivers.
NLT.26:
And he will call out to me, ‘You are my Father,
my God, and the Rock of my salvation.’
NLT.27:
I will make him my firstborn son,
the mightiest king on earth.
NLT.28:
I will love him and be kind to him forever;
my covenant with him will never end.
NLT.29:
I will preserve an heir for him;
his throne will be as endless as the days of heaven.
NLT.30:
But if his descendants forsake my instructions
and fail to obey my regulations,
NLT.31:
if they do not obey my decrees
and fail to keep my commands,
NLT.32:
then I will punish their sin with the rod,
and their disobedience with beating.
NLT.33:
But I will never stop loving him
nor fail to keep my promise to him.
NLT.34:
No, I will not break my covenant;
I will not take back a single word I said.
NLT.35:
I have sworn an oath to David,
and in my holiness I cannot lie:
NLT.36:
His dynasty will go on forever;
his kingdom will endure as the sun.
NLT.37:
It will be as eternal as the moon,
my faithful witness in the sky!” Interlude
NLT.38:
But now you have rejected him and cast him off.
You are angry with your anointed king.
NLT.39:
You have renounced your covenant with him;
you have thrown his crown in the dust.
NLT.40:
You have broken down the walls protecting him
and ruined every fort defending him.
NLT.41:
Everyone who comes along has robbed him,
and he has become a joke to his neighbors.
NLT.42:
You have strengthened his enemies
and made them all rejoice.
NLT.43:
You have made his sword useless
and refused to help him in battle.
NLT.44:
You have ended his splendor
and overturned his throne.
NLT.45:
You have made him old before his time
and publicly disgraced him. Interlude
NLT.46:
O LORD, how long will this go on?
Will you hide yourself forever?
How long will your anger burn like fire?
NLT.47:
Remember how short my life is,
how empty and futile this human existence!
NLT.48:
No one can live forever; all will die.
No one can escape the power of the grave. Interlude
NLT.49:
Lord, where is your unfailing love?
You promised it to David with a faithful pledge.
NLT.50:
Consider, Lord, how your servants are disgraced!
I carry in my heart the insults of so many people.
NLT.51:
Your enemies have mocked me, O LORD;
they mock your anointed king wherever he goes.
NLT.52:
Praise the LORD forever!
Amen and amen!

WEB.1: A contemplation by Ethan, the Ezrahite. I will sing of the loving kindness of Yahweh forever. With my mouth, I will make known your faithfulness to all generations.
WEB.2: I indeed declare, “Love stands firm forever. You established the heavens. Your faithfulness is in them.”
WEB.3: “I have made a covenant with my chosen one, I have sworn to David, my servant,
WEB.4: ‘I will establish your offspring forever, and build up your throne to all generations.’” Selah.
WEB.5: The heavens will praise your wonders, Yahweh; your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.
WEB.6: For who in the skies can be compared to Yahweh? Who among the sons of the heavenly beings is like Yahweh,
WEB.7: a very awesome God in the council of the holy ones, to be feared above all those who are around him?
WEB.8: Yahweh, God of Armies, who is a mighty one, like you? Yah, your faithfulness is around you.
WEB.9: You rule the pride of the sea. When its waves rise up, you calm them.
WEB.10: You have broken Rahab in pieces, like one of the slain. You have scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.
WEB.11: The heavens are yours. The earth also is yours; the world and its fullness. You have founded them.
WEB.12: The north and the south, you have created them. Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name.
WEB.13: You have a mighty arm. Your hand is strong, and your right hand is exalted.
WEB.14: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne. Loving kindness and truth go before your face.
WEB.15: Blessed are the people who learn to acclaim you. They walk in the light of your presence, Yahweh.
WEB.16: In your name they rejoice all day. In your righteousness, they are exalted.
WEB.17: For you are the glory of their strength. In your favor, our horn will be exalted.
WEB.18: For our shield belongs to Yahweh; our king to the Holy One of Israel.
WEB.19: Then you spoke in vision to your saints, and said, “I have given strength to the warrior. I have exalted a young man from the people.
WEB.20: I have found David, my servant. I have anointed him with my holy oil,
WEB.21: with whom my hand shall be established. My arm will also strengthen him.
WEB.22: No enemy will tax him. No wicked man will oppress him.
WEB.23: I will beat down his adversaries before him, and strike those who hate him.
WEB.24: But my faithfulness and my loving kindness will be with him. In my name, his horn will be exalted.
WEB.25: I will set his hand also on the sea, and his right hand on the rivers.
WEB.26: He will call to me, ‘You are my Father, my God, and the rock of my salvation!’
WEB.27: I will also appoint him my firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.
WEB.28: I will keep my loving kindness for him forever more. My covenant will stand firm with him.
WEB.29: I will also make his offspring  endure forever, and his throne as the days of heaven.
WEB.30: If his children forsake my law, and don’t walk in my ordinances;
WEB.31: if they break my statutes, and don’t keep my commandments;
WEB.32: then I will punish their sin with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.
WEB.33: But I will not completely take my loving kindness from him, nor allow my faithfulness to fail.
WEB.34: I will not break my covenant, nor alter what my lips have uttered.
WEB.35: Once I have sworn by my holiness, I will not lie to David.
WEB.36: His offspring  will endure forever, his throne like the sun before me.
WEB.37: It will be established forever like the moon, the faithful witness in the sky.” Selah.
WEB.38: But you have rejected and spurned. You have been angry with your anointed.
WEB.39: You have renounced the covenant of your servant. You have defiled his crown in the dust.
WEB.40: You have broken down all his hedges. You have brought his strongholds to ruin.
WEB.41: All who pass by the way rob him. He has become a reproach to his neighbors.
WEB.42: You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries. You have made all of his enemies rejoice.
WEB.43: Yes, you turn back the edge of his sword, and haven’t supported him in battle.
WEB.44: You have ended his splendor, and thrown his throne down to the ground.
WEB.45: You have shortened the days of his youth. You have covered him with shame. Selah.
WEB.46: How long, Yahweh? Will you hide yourself forever? Will your wrath burn like fire?
WEB.47: Remember how short my time is! For what vanity have you created all the children of men!
WEB.48: What man is he who shall live and not see death, who shall deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah.
WEB.49: Lord, where are your former loving kindnesses, which you swore to David in your faithfulness?
WEB.50: Remember, Lord, the reproach of your servants, how I bear in my heart the taunts of all the mighty peoples,
WEB.51: With which your enemies have mocked, Yahweh, with which they have mocked the footsteps of your anointed one.
WEB.52: Blessed be Yahweh forever more. Amen, and Amen.

AMP.1:
A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of Ethan the Ezrahite. I will sing of the goodness and lovingkindness of the LORD forever;
With my mouth I will make known Your faithfulness from generation to generation.
AMP.2:
For I have said, "Goodness and lovingkindness will be built up forever;
In the heavens [unchangeable and majestic] You will establish Your faithfulness."
AMP.3:
[God has said] "I have made a covenant with My chosen one;
I have sworn to David My servant,
AMP.4:
I will establish your seed forever
And I will build up your throne for all generations." Selah.
AMP.5:
The heavens (angels) praise Your wonders, O LORD,
Your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.
AMP.6:
For who in the heavens can be compared to the LORD?
Who among the divine beings is like the LORD,
AMP.7:
A God greatly feared and reverently worshiped in the council of the holy [angelic] ones,
And awesome above all those who are around Him?
AMP.8:
O LORD God of hosts, who is like You, O mighty LORD?
Your faithfulness surrounds You [as an intrinsic, unchangeable part of Your very being].
AMP.9:
You rule the swelling of the sea;
When its waves rise, You still them.
AMP.10:
You have crushed Rahab (Egypt) like one who is slain;
You have scattered Your enemies with Your mighty arm.
AMP.11:
The heavens are Yours, the earth also is Yours;
The world and all that is in it, You have founded and established them.
AMP.12:
The north and the south, You have created them;
Mount Tabor and Mount Hermon shout for joy at Your name.
AMP.13:
You have a strong arm;
Mighty is Your hand, Your right hand is exalted.
AMP.14:
Righteousness and justice are the foundation of Your throne;
Lovingkindness and truth go before You.
AMP.15:
Blessed and happy are the people who know the joyful sound [of the trumpet's blast]!
They walk, O LORD, in the light and favor of Your countenance!
AMP.16:
In Your name they rejoice all the day,
And in Your righteousness they are exalted.
AMP.17:
For You are the glory of their strength [their proud adornment],
And by Your favor our horn is exalted.
AMP.18:
For our shield belongs to the LORD,
And our king to the Holy One of Israel.
AMP.19:
Once You spoke in a vision to Your godly ones,
And said, "I have given help to one who is mighty [giving him the power to be a champion for Israel];
I have exalted one chosen from the people.
AMP.20:
"I have found David My servant;
With My holy oil I have anointed him,
AMP.21:
With whom My hand shall be established and steadfast;
My arm also shall strengthen him.
AMP.22:
"The enemy will not outwit him,
Nor will the wicked man afflict or humiliate him.
AMP.23:
"I will crush his adversaries before him,
And strike those who hate him.
AMP.24:
"My faithfulness and My steadfast lovingkindness shall be with him,
And in My name shall his horn be exalted [great power and prosperity shall be conferred upon him].
AMP.25:
"I will also set his hand on the [Mediterranean] sea,
And his right hand on the rivers [the tributaries of the Euphrates].
AMP.26:
"He will cry to Me, 'You are my Father,
My God, and the rock of my salvation.'
AMP.27:
"I will also make him My firstborn (preeminent),
The highest of the kings of the earth.
AMP.28:
"My lovingkindness I will keep for him forevermore,
And My covenant will be confirmed to him.
AMP.29:
"His descendants I will establish forever,
And his throne [will endure] as the days of heaven.
AMP.30:
"If his children turn away from My law
And do not walk in My ordinances,
AMP.31:
If they break My statutes
And do not keep My commandments,
AMP.32:
Then I will punish their transgression with the rod [of discipline],
And [correct] their wickedness with stripes.
AMP.33:
"Nevertheless, I will not break off My lovingkindness from him,
Nor allow My faithfulness to fail.
AMP.34:
"My covenant I will not violate,
Nor will I alter the utterance of My lips.
AMP.35:
"Once [for all] I have sworn by My holiness, [My vow which cannot be violated];
I will not lie to David.
AMP.36:
"His descendants shall endure forever
And his throne [will continue] as the sun before Me.
AMP.37:
"It shall be established forever like the moon,
And the witness in the heavens is ever faithful." Selah.
AMP.38:
But [in apparent contradiction of all this] You [the faithful LORD] have cast off and rejected;
You have been full of wrath against Your anointed.
AMP.39:
You have spurned and repudiated the covenant with Your servant;
You have profaned his crown [by casting it] in the dust.
AMP.40:
You have broken down all his [city] walls;
You have brought his strongholds to ruin.
AMP.41:
All who pass along the road rob him;
He has become the scorn of his neighbors.
AMP.42:
You have exalted the right hand of his foes;
You have made all his enemies rejoice.
AMP.43:
Also, You have turned back the edge of his sword
And have not made him [strong enough] to stand in battle.
AMP.44:
You have put an end to his splendor
And have hurled his throne to the ground.
AMP.45:
You have shortened the days of his youth;
You have covered him with shame. Selah.
AMP.46:
How long, O LORD?
Will You hide Yourself forever?
Will Your wrath burn like fire?
AMP.47:
Remember how fleeting my lifetime is;
For what vanity, [for what emptiness, for what futility, for what wisp of smoke] You have created all the sons of men!
AMP.48:
What man can live and not see death?
Can he rescue his soul from the [powerful] hand of Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead)? Selah.
AMP.49:
O Lord, where are Your former lovingkindnesses [so abundant in the days of David and Solomon],
Which You swore to David in Your faithfulness?
AMP.50:
Remember, O Lord, the reproach of Your servants [scorned, insulted, and disgraced];
How I bear in my heart the reproach of all the many peoples,
AMP.51:
With which Your enemies have taunted, O LORD,
With which they have mocked the footsteps of Your anointed.
AMP.52:
Blessed be the LORD forevermore!
Amen and Amen.

MSG.1:
An Ethan prayer
Your love, God, is my song, and I'll sing it!
I'm forever telling everyone how faithful you are.
MSG.2:
I'll never quit telling the story of your love—
how you built the cosmos
and guaranteed everything in it.
Your love has always been our lives' foundation,
your fidelity has been the roof over our world.
MSG.3:
You once said, "I joined forces with my chosen leader,
I pledged my word to my servant, David, saying,
MSG.4:
'Everyone descending from you is guaranteed life;
I'll make your rule as solid and lasting as rock.' "
MSG.5:
God! Let the cosmos praise your wonderful ways,
the choir of holy angels sing anthems to your faithful ways!
MSG.6:
Search high and low, scan skies and land,
you'll find nothing and no one quite like God.
MSG.7:
The holy angels are in awe before him;
he looms immense and august over everyone around him.
MSG.8:
God of the Angel Armies, who is like you,
powerful and faithful from every angle?
MSG.9:
You put the arrogant ocean in its place
and calm its waves when they turn unruly.
MSG.10:
You gave that old hag Egypt the back of your hand,
you brushed off your enemies with a flick of your wrist.
MSG.11:
You own the cosmos—you made everything in it,
everything from atom to archangel.
MSG.12:
You positioned the North and South Poles;
the mountains Tabor and Hermon sing duets to you.
MSG.13:
With your well-muscled arm and your grip of steel—
nobody trifles with you!
MSG.14:
The Right and Justice are the roots of your rule;
Love and Truth are its fruits.
MSG.15:
Blessed are the people who know the passwords of praise,
who shout on parade in the bright presence of God.
MSG.16:
Delighted, they dance all day long; they know
who you are, what you do—they can't keep it quiet!
MSG.17:
Your vibrant beauty has gotten inside us—
you've been so good to us! We're walking on air!
MSG.18:
All we are and have we owe to God,
Holy God of Israel, our King!
MSG.19:
A long time ago you spoke in a vision,
you spoke to your faithful beloved:
"I've crowned a hero,
I chose the best I could find;
MSG.20:
I found David, my servant,
poured holy oil on his head,
MSG.21:
And I'll keep my hand steadily on him,
yes, I'll stick with him through thick and thin.
MSG.22:
No enemy will get the best of him,
no scoundrel will do him in.
MSG.23:
I'll weed out all who oppose him,
I'll clean out all who hate him.
MSG.24:
I'm with him for good and I'll love him forever;
I've set him on high—he's riding high!
MSG.25: I've put Ocean in his one hand, River in the other;
MSG.26: he'll call out, 'Oh, my Father—my God, my Rock of Salvation!'
MSG.27:
Yes, I'm setting him apart as the First of the royal line,
High King over all of earth's kings.
MSG.28:
I'll preserve him eternally in my love,
I'll faithfully do all I so solemnly promised.
MSG.29:
I'll guarantee his family tree
and underwrite his rule.
MSG.30:
If his children refuse to do what I tell them,
if they refuse to walk in the way I show them,
MSG.31:
If they spit on the directions I give them
and tear up the rules I post for them
MSG.32:
—I'll rub their faces in the dirt of their rebellion
and make them face the music.
MSG.33:
But I'll never throw them out,
never abandon or disown them.
MSG.34:
Do you think I'd withdraw my holy promise?
or take back words I'd already spoken?
MSG.35:
I've given my word, my whole and holy word;
do you think I would lie to David?
MSG.36:
His family tree is here for good,
his sovereignty as sure as the sun,
MSG.37:
Dependable as the phases of the moon,
inescapable as weather."
MSG.38:
But God, you did walk off and leave us,
you lost your temper with the one you anointed.
MSG.39:
You tore up the promise you made to your servant,
you stomped his crown in the mud.
MSG.40:
You blasted his home to kingdom come,
reduced his city to a pile of rubble
MSG.41:
Picked clean by wayfaring strangers,
a joke to all the neighbors.
MSG.42:
You declared a holiday for all his enemies,

MSG.43:
Angry, you opposed him in battle,

MSG.44:
You robbed him of his splendor, humiliated this warrior,

MSG.45:
You took the best years of his life

MSG.46:

NIV.1:
Psalm 89
A maskil of Ethan the Ezrahite.
I will sing of the Lord’s great love forever;
with my mouth I will make your faithfulness known through all generations.
NIV.2:
I will declare that your love stands firm forever,
that you established your faithfulness in heaven itself.
NIV.3:
You said, “I have made a covenant with my chosen one,
I have sworn to David my servant,
NIV.4:
‘I will establish your line forever
and make your throne firm through all generations.’” Selah
NIV.5:
The heavens praise your wonders, O Lord,
your faithfulness too, in the assembly of the holy ones.
NIV.6:
For who in the skies above can compare with the Lord?
Who is like the Lord among the heavenly beings?
NIV.7:
In the council of the holy ones God is greatly feared;
he is more awesome than all who surround him.
NIV.8:
O Lord God Almighty, who is like you?
You are mighty, O Lord, and your faithfulness surrounds you.
NIV.9:
You rule over the surging sea;
when its waves mount up, you still them.
NIV.10:
You crushed Rahab like one of the slain;
with your strong arm you scattered your enemies.
NIV.11:
The heavens are yours, and yours also the earth;
you founded the world and all that is in it.
NIV.12:
You created the north and the south;
Tabor and Hermon sing for joy at your name.
NIV.13:
Your arm is endued with power;
your hand is strong, your right hand exalted.
NIV.14:
Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne;
love and faithfulness go before you.
NIV.15:
Blessed are those who have learned to acclaim you,
who walk in the light of your presence, O Lord.
NIV.16:
They rejoice in your name all day long;
they exult in your righteousness.
NIV.17:
For you are their glory and strength,
and by your favor you exalt our horn.
NIV.18:
Indeed, our shield belongs to the Lord,
our king to the Holy One of Israel.
NIV.19:
Once you spoke in a vision,
to your faithful people you said:
“I have bestowed strength on a warrior;
I have exalted a young man from among the people.
NIV.20:
I have found David my servant;
with my sacred oil I have anointed him.
NIV.21:
My hand will sustain him;
surely my arm will strengthen him.
NIV.22:
No enemy will subject him to tribute;
no wicked man will oppress him.
NIV.23:
I will crush his foes before him
and strike down his adversaries.
NIV.24:
My faithful love will be with him,
and through my name his horn will be exalted.
NIV.25:
I will set his hand over the sea,
his right hand over the rivers.
NIV.26:
He will call out to me, ‘You are my Father,
my God, the Rock my Savior.’
NIV.27:
I will also appoint him my firstborn,
the most exalted of the kings of the earth.
NIV.28:
I will maintain my love to him forever,
and my covenant with him will never fail.
NIV.29:
I will establish his line forever,
his throne as long as the heavens endure.
NIV.30:
“If his sons forsake my law
and do not follow my statutes,
NIV.31:
if they violate my decrees
and fail to keep my commands,
NIV.32:
I will punish their sin with the rod,
their iniquity with flogging;
NIV.33:
but I will not take my love from him,
nor will I ever betray my faithfulness.
NIV.34:
I will not violate my covenant
or alter what my lips have uttered.
NIV.35:
Once for all, I have sworn by my holiness—
and I will not lie to David—
NIV.36:
that his line will continue forever
and his throne endure before me like the sun;
NIV.37:
it will be established forever like the moon,
the faithful witness in the sky.” Selah
NIV.38:
But you have rejected, you have spurned,
you have been very angry with your anointed one.
NIV.39:
You have renounced the covenant with your servant
and have defiled his crown in the dust.
NIV.40:
You have broken through all his walls
and reduced his strongholds to ruins.
NIV.41:
All who pass by have plundered him;
he has become the scorn of his neighbors.
NIV.42:
You have exalted the right hand of his foes;
you have made all his enemies rejoice.
NIV.43:
You have turned back the edge of his sword
and have not supported him in battle.
NIV.44:
You have put an end to his splendor
and cast his throne to the ground.
NIV.45:
You have cut short the days of his youth;
you have covered him with a mantle of shame. Selah
NIV.46:
How long, O Lord? Will you hide yourself forever?
How long will your wrath burn like fire?
NIV.47:
Remember how fleeting is my life.
For what futility you have created all men!
NIV.48:
What man can live and not see death,
or save himself from the power of the grave? Selah
NIV.49:
O Lord, where is your former great love,
which in your faithfulness you swore to David?
NIV.50:
Remember, Lord, how your servant has been mocked,
how I bear in my heart the taunts of all the nations,
NIV.51:
the taunts with which your enemies have mocked, O Lord,
with which they have mocked every step of your anointed one.
NIV.52:
Praise be to the Lord forever!
Amen and Amen.

NRSVCE.1:
I will sing of your steadfast love, O LORD, forever;
with my mouth I will proclaim your faithfulness to all generations.
NRSVCE.2:
I declare that your steadfast love is established forever;
your faithfulness is as firm as the heavens.
NRSVCE.3:
You said, “I have made a covenant with my chosen one,

NRSVCE.4:
‘I will establish your descendants forever,

NRSVCE.5:
Let the heavens praise your wonders, O LORD,

NRSVCE.6:
For who in the skies can be compared to the LORD?
Who among the heavenly beings is like the LORD,
NRSVCE.7:
a God feared in the council of the holy ones,

NRSVCE.8:
O LORD God of hosts,

Your faithfulness surrounds you.
NRSVCE.9:
You rule the raging of the sea;

NRSVCE.10:
You crushed Rahab like a carcass;

NRSVCE.11:
The heavens are yours, the earth also is yours;

NRSVCE.12:
The north and the south — you created them;
Tabor and Hermon joyously praise your name.
NRSVCE.13:
You have a mighty arm;

NRSVCE.14:
Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne;

NRSVCE.15:
Happy are the people who know the festal shout,

NRSVCE.16:

NRSVCE.17:
For you are the glory of their strength;

NRSVCE.18:
For our shield belongs to the LORD,
our king to the Holy One of Israel.
NRSVCE.19:
Then you spoke in a vision to your faithful one, and said:
“I have set the crown on one who is mighty,
I have exalted one chosen from the people.
NRSVCE.20:
I have found my servant David;
with my holy oil I have anointed him;

NRSVCE.22:
The enemy shall not outwit him,
the wicked shall not humble him.
NRSVCE.23:
I will crush his foes before him
and strike down those who hate him.
NRSVCE.24:
My faithfulness and steadfast love shall be with him;
and in my name his horn shall be exalted.
NRSVCE.25:
I will set his hand on the sea
and his right hand on the rivers.
NRSVCE.26:
He shall cry to me, ‘You are my Father,
my God, and the Rock of my salvation!’
NRSVCE.27:
I will make him the firstborn,
the highest of the kings of the earth.
NRSVCE.28:
Forever I will keep my steadfast love for him,
and my covenant with him will stand firm.
NRSVCE.29:
I will establish his line forever,

NRSVCE.30:
If his children forsake my law
and do not walk according to my ordinances,
NRSVCE.31:
if they violate my statutes
and do not keep my commandments,
NRSVCE.32:
then I will punish their transgression with the rod
and their iniquity with scourges;
NRSVCE.33:
but I will not remove from him my steadfast love,
or be false to my faithfulness.
NRSVCE.34:
I will not violate my covenant,
or alter the word that went forth from my lips.
NRSVCE.35:
Once and for all I have sworn by my holiness;
I will not lie to David.
NRSVCE.36:
His line shall continue forever,
and his throne endure before me like the sun.
NRSVCE.37:
It shall be established forever like the moon,
an enduring witness in the skies.” Selah
NRSVCE.38:
But now you have spurned and rejected him;
you are full of wrath against your anointed.
NRSVCE.39:
You have renounced the covenant with your servant;
you have defiled his crown in the dust.
NRSVCE.40:
You have broken through all his walls;
you have laid his strongholds in ruins.
NRSVCE.41:
All who pass by plunder him;
he has become the scorn of his neighbors.
NRSVCE.42:
You have exalted the right hand of his foes;

NRSVCE.43:

NRSVCE.44:
You have removed the scepter from his hand,
and hurled his throne to the ground.
NRSVCE.45:
You have cut short the days of his youth;
you have covered him with shame. Selah
NRSVCE.46:
How long, O LORD? Will you hide yourself forever?
How long will your wrath burn like fire?
NRSVCE.47:
Remember how short my time is —
for what vanity you have created all mortals!
NRSVCE.48:
Who can live and never see death?

NRSVCE.49:

NRSVCE.50:
Remember, O Lord, how your servant is taunted;
how I bear in my bosom the insults of the peoples,
NRSVCE.51:

with which they taunted the footsteps of your anointed.
NRSVCE.52: Blessed be the LORD forever. Amen and Amen.

NET.1: I will sing continually about the LORD’s faithful deeds; to future generations I will proclaim your faithfulness.
NET.2: For I say, “Loyal love is permanently established; in the skies you set up your faithfulness.”
NET.3: The LORD said, “I have made a covenant with my chosen one; I have made a promise on oath to David, my servant:
NET.4: ‘I will give you an eternal dynasty and establish your throne throughout future generations.’” (Selah)
NET.5: O LORD, the heavens praise your amazing deeds, as well as your faithfulness in the angelic assembly.
NET.6: For who in the skies can compare to the LORD? Who is like the LORD among the heavenly beings,
NET.7: a God who is honored in the great angelic assembly, and more awesome than all who surround him?
NET.8: O LORD, sovereign God! Who is strong like you, O LORD? Your faithfulness surrounds you.
NET.9: You rule over the proud sea. When its waves surge, you calm them.
NET.10: You crushed the Proud One and killed it; with your strong arm you scattered your enemies.
NET.11: The heavens belong to you, as does the earth. You made the world and all it contains.
NET.12: You created the north and the south. Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name.
NET.13: Your arm is powerful, your hand strong, your right hand victorious.
NET.14: Equity and justice are the foundation of your throne. Loyal love and faithfulness characterize your rule.
NET.15: How blessed are the people who worship you! O LORD, they experience your favor.
NET.16: They rejoice in your name all day long, and are vindicated by your justice.
NET.17: For you give them splendor and strength. By your favor we are victorious.
NET.18: For our shield belongs to the LORD, our king to the Holy One of Israel.
NET.19: Then you spoke through a vision to your faithful followers and said: “I have energized a warrior; I have raised up a young man from the people.
NET.20: I have discovered David, my servant. With my holy oil I have anointed him as king.
NET.21: My hand will support him, and my arm will strengthen him.
NET.22: No enemy will be able to exact tribute from him; a violent oppressor will not be able to humiliate him.
NET.23: I will crush his enemies before him; I will strike down those who hate him.
NET.24: He will experience my faithfulness and loyal love, and by my name he will win victories.
NET.25: I will place his hand over the sea, his right hand over the rivers.
NET.26: He will call out to me, ‘You are my father, my God, and the protector who delivers me.’
NET.27: I will appoint him to be my firstborn son, the most exalted of the earth’s kings.
NET.28: I will always extend my loyal love to him, and my covenant with him is secure.
NET.29: I will give him an eternal dynasty, and make his throne as enduring as the skies above.
NET.30: If his sons reject my law and disobey my regulations,
NET.31: if they break my rules and do not keep my commandments,
NET.32: I will punish their rebellion by beating them with a club, their sin by inflicting them with bruises.
NET.33: But I will not remove my loyal love from him, nor be unfaithful to my promise.
NET.34: I will not break my covenant or go back on what I promised.
NET.35: Once and for all I have vowed by my own holiness, I will never deceive David.
NET.36: His dynasty will last forever. His throne will endure before me, like the sun,
NET.37: it will remain stable, like the moon, his throne will endure like the skies.” (Selah)
NET.38: But you have spurned and rejected him; you are angry with your chosen king.
NET.39: You have repudiated your covenant with your servant; you have thrown his crown to the ground.
NET.40: You have broken down all his walls; you have made his strongholds a heap of ruins.
NET.41: All who pass by have robbed him; he has become an object of disdain to his neighbors.
NET.42: You have allowed his adversaries to be victorious, and all his enemies to rejoice.
NET.43: You turn back his sword from the adversary, and have not sustained him in battle.
NET.44: You have brought to an end his splendor, and have knocked his throne to the ground.
NET.45: You have cut short his youth, and have covered him with shame. (Selah)
NET.46: How long, O LORD, will this last? Will you remain hidden forever? Will your anger continue to burn like fire?
NET.47: Take note of my brief lifespan! Why do you make all people so mortal?
NET.48: No man can live on without experiencing death, or deliver his life from the power of Sheol. (Selah)
NET.49: Where are your earlier faithful deeds, O Lord, the ones performed in accordance with your reliable oath to David?
NET.50: Take note, O Lord, of the way your servants are taunted, and of how I must bear so many insults from people!
NET.51: Your enemies, O LORD, hurl insults; they insult your chosen king as they dog his footsteps.
NET.52: The LORD deserves praise forevermore! We agree! We agree!
